--- title: "Multi-language Quarto: R + Python" author: "Z. Wang" format: html: toc: true code-fold: true execute: echo: true warning: false message: false --- ## Markdown + Inline Math You can type markdown freely, and LaTex style inline math like $\mu$, $\sigma^2$, or display math like $\mu$ $a + b \neq b + a$ $$ log(\frac{p}{1-p})=\beta_0 + \beta_1x_1 + \beta_2 x_2 $$ **Bold**, *italic,* [**hyperlink**](https://my.denison.edu/), list, table, figure, citation, etc. just like in normal Markdown. - bullet 1 - bullet 2 | Col1 | Col2 | Col3 | |------|------|------| | 1 | 1 | | | 2 | 2 | | | 3 | 3 | | ## Figure ![Adam](Adam.png) ## R chunk ::: {.cell} ```{.r .cell-code} # R code example library(ggplot2) ggplot(data = cars, aes(x = speed, y = dist)) + geom_point(color = "blue", size = 3, alpha = 0.8) + geom_smooth(method = "lm",linetype = "dashed", color = "firebrick") + labs( title = "Stopping Distance vs. Speed", x = "Speed (mph)", y = "Stopping Distance (ft)" ) ``` ::: {.cell-output-display} ![](200_files/figure-html/unnamed-chunk-1-1.png){width=672} ::: ::: ::: {.cell} ```{.r .cell-code} # call python library(reticulate) py_config() ``` ::: {.cell-output .cell-output-stdout} ``` python: /Users/wangz/.virtualenvs/r-reticulate/bin/python libpython: /Library/Frameworks/Python.framework/Versions/3.11/lib/python3.11/config-3.11-darwin/libpython3.11.dylib pythonhome: /Users/wangz/.virtualenvs/r-reticulate:/Users/wangz/.virtualenvs/r-reticulate version: 3.11.3 (v3.11.3:f3909b8bc8, Apr 4 2023, 20:12:10) [Clang 13.0.0 (clang-1300.0.29.30)] numpy: /Users/wangz/.virtualenvs/r-reticulate/lib/python3.11/site-packages/numpy numpy_version: 2.3.3 ``` ::: ```{.r .cell-code} py_install("pandas") # install python package in r ``` ::: {.cell-output .cell-output-stdout} ``` Using virtual environment '/Users/wangz/.virtualenvs/r-reticulate' ... ``` ::: ```{.r .cell-code} py_install("matplotlib") ``` ::: {.cell-output .cell-output-stdout} ``` Using virtual environment '/Users/wangz/.virtualenvs/r-reticulate' ... ``` ::: ::: ## Python chunk: ::: {.cell} ```{.python .cell-code} import pandas as pd import numpy as np import matplotlib.pyplot as plt # small df df = pd.DataFrame({"x": np.arange(5), "y": [3,1,4,1,5]}) # Create a basic line plot plt.plot(df.x, df.y, marker="o") plt.xlabel("x") plt.ylabel("y") plt.title("Line Plot Example") # Show the plot window plt.show() ``` ::: {.cell-output-display} ![](200_files/figure-html/unnamed-chunk-3-1.png){width=672} ::: ::: ## Transfer Data between R and Python Write and Read ::: {.cell} ```{.r .cell-code} # write.csv(cars, "cars.csv", row.names = FALSE) ``` ::: ::: {.cell} ```{.python .cell-code} import pandas as pd data = pd.read_csv("cars.csv") data.head() ``` ::: {.cell-output-display} ```{=html}
speed dist
0 4 2
1 4 10
2 7 4
3 7 22
4 8 16
``` ::: ::: Or direct send your object to Python ::: {.cell} ```{.r .cell-code} # Send to Python py$cars <- cars # assign to Python ``` ::: check function `r_to_python` and `python_to_r`
